description: Emerging semantic signals under watch for Vortik. These are not canonical anchors and should not be treated as protocol-stable registry primitives yet.

signals.name: Block Access List
signals.summary: Execution-layer coordination object under watch as Ethereum execution research moves toward block-level access descriptions, sidecars, state healing, proposer commitments, and execution-resource decomposition.
why_it_matters: Signals movement toward narrower execution-layer terminology rather than broad market abstractions.
May pressure umbrella names such as executionmarket.eth and blockspacemarket.eth.
Could become a durable execution-facing semantic surface if protocol and client language continues converging around BAL terminology.

signals.name: Payload Timeliness Committee
signals.summary: Protocol-facing committee concept associated with ePBS timing, payload reveal accountability, and builder/proposer coordination under enshrined block building.
why_it_matters: Directly relates to ePBS coordination and payload reveal timing.
May become an important sub-surface around builder accountability.
Strengthens the need to track roles beneath the main ePBS primitive.

signals.name: LUCID
signals.summary: Protocol-facing encrypted-orderflow signal connecting inclusion guarantees, commit-before-reveal flow, and MEV-sensitive transaction protection.
why_it_matters: Connects inclusion guarantees, encrypted order flow, and commit-before-reveal execution.
May strengthen the semantic importance of inclusion lists as a public-facing primitive.
Introduces pressure toward more protocol-native vocabulary than generic OFA framing.

signals.name: Execution Tickets
signals.summary: Forward-looking execution-coordination term under watch as Ethereum execution design may continue decomposing into narrower protocol-facing objects.
why_it_matters: Could become strategically important if protocol discussion converges around the term.
Still too early for canonical treatment.

signals.name: Single Slot Finality
signals.summary: Finality roadmap concept under watch as SSF terminology may continue to displace broader fast-finality language.
why_it_matters: May pressure fastfinality.eth if SSF remains the dominant exact term.
Relevant to long-term Ethereum roadmap semantics.
Should remain watchlisted until its roadmap status becomes more concrete.

signals.name: Key Provider
signals.summary: Potential future coordination role relevant to encrypted mempool or threshold decryption systems if protected order flow becomes more protocol-shaped.
why_it_matters: Could emerge as a meaningful role if encrypted transaction flow becomes more formalized.
Represents a possible future semantic surface around protected order flow.
